title: 10 best herbal teas for frozen shoulder

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Danshen teas helps with frozen shoulder because it contains Salvia miltiorrhiza, a plant-based compound that reduces inflammation and promotes blood flow. As frozen shoulder is characterized by stiffness, pain, and limited mobility due to reduced blood supply and inflammation, the anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties of danshen tea may help alleviate these symptoms. By improving circulation and reducing swelling, danshen tea may also aid in the healing process, allowing for greater range of motion and relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Danshen tea's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and pain associated with frozen shoulder, making it easier to move and perform daily activities.
Analgesic property: The analgesic property of Danshen tea can help relieve pain and discomfort caused by frozen shoulder, allowing patients to manage their symptoms more effectively.

Blood circulation promoting property: By improving blood circulation, Danshen tea may help to reduce stiffness and improve range of motion in the affected shoulder joint, reducing the severity of frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, danshen roots are used to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, making them effective in treating frozen shoulder.
Rhyzomes: Containing saponins and other active constituents, rhyzomes of danshen plant help alleviate pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried danshen root in quantities of 30g per serving.
Boil 500ml water and steep the danshen root for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 5-10g of dried ginger slices to enhance digestion and reduce nausea.
Allow the tea to cool before drinking within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet annie teas helps with frozen shoulder because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. The tea's flavonoids and polyphenols also improve blood circulation, which increases oxygen and nutrient supply to the muscles and tendons, promoting healing and relaxation. Additionally, sweet annie's sedative properties help alleviate stress and anxiety, common underlying causes of frozen shoulder. By addressing these factors, sweet annie teas can provide relief from stiffness, pain, and limited mobility associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sweet Annie tea contains artemisinin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, which can be beneficial in reducing stiffness and discomfort associated with Frozen Shoulder.
Analgesic: The tea's analgesic properties can help relieve pain and discomfort in the affected shoulder joint, making it easier to move and perform daily activities.

Antioxidant: Sweet Annie tea's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress and damage caused by inflammation, which may aid in reducing scar tissue formation and improving range of motion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, which help reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with Frozen Shoulder.
Barks: The barks contain alkaloids that can help relax muscles and improve joint mobility, making them effective in treating the stiffness and rigidity of Frozen Shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried artemisia annua leaves per eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the herb in the hot water for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids after the steeping time has elapsed.
Add one tablespoon of honey or other sweetener to taste and stir well.
Drink the tea three times a day as needed or directed by a healthcare provider.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yanhusuo teas helps with frozen shoulder because it contains a unique blend of herbs that promote blood circulation, reduce inflammation, and relax tense muscles. The tea's warming properties help to loosen stiff joints, allowing for greater mobility and flexibility. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory compounds in Yanhusuo teas may help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder, making it an effective natural remedy for relief and recovery. Regular consumption of Yanhusuo teas may also help to prevent future episodes of frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties: Yanhusuo tea has been shown to reduce inflammation and pain in the affected area, which is essential for relieving the stiffness and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.

Muscle relaxant properties: The active compounds in Yanhusuo tea can help relax tense muscles, improve joint mobility, and reduce muscle spasms, making it an effective treatment for frozen shoulder symptoms like limited range of motion and pain with movement.

Antioxidant and free radical scavenging properties: Yanhusuo tea's antioxidant properties can help protect the affected joints from oxidative damage caused by inflammation, promoting healing and reducing scar tissue formation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in starch and mucilage, making it an effective ingredient to soothe pain and reduce inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Contains sesquiterpenes and alkaloids that help to relieve stiffness and promote relaxation of the affected muscles.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried corydalis yanhusuo root and 1 teaspoon of dried angelica dahurica root into a small bowl.
Boil 2 cups of pure water in a teapot for 5 minutes before reducing heat to simmer.
Steep the herbal mixture in the boiling water for 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors to infuse and combine.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup, discarding any solids and leaving only the liquid extract.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming, sipping on it warm or at room temperature throughout the day as needed for pain relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai teas helps with frozen shoulder because of its ability to promote blood circulation, reduce inflammation, and relieve pain. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to ease stiffness and discomfort in the affected area, while its warming effects can increase range of motion and flexibility. Additionally, dong quai's ability to balance hormones and regulate bodily fluids may also help alleviate symptoms associated with frozen shoulder, such as numbness or tingling sensations. By drinking dong quai tea regularly, individuals with frozen shoulder may experience significant relief from their condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties: Dong Quai's compounds, such as ferulic acid and ligustilide, can reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in affected joints, providing relief from Frozen Shoulder symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Dong Quai tea help protect the body against oxidative stress, which may contribute to joint degeneration and inflammation associated with Frozen Shoulder.

Blood flow promotion: The herb's ability to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation can help increase oxygenation and nutrient supply to the affected shoulder area, promoting healing and reducing stiffness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, flavonoids, and polysaccharides, which help reduce inflammation and improve joint mobility.
Rhyzomes: Containing a unique combination of active compounds, rhyzomes have been traditionally used to alleviate pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried angelica sinensis roots and measure out 2 teaspoons per cup of boiling water.
Bring the measured water to a boil in a medium-sized pot then reduce heat to simmer.
Add the 2 teaspoons of dried dong quai roots to the simmering water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a large mug and add honey or lemon to taste if desired after cooling slightly.
Allow the tea to cool completely before refrigerating it for up to 3 days or freezing it for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese birthwort teas helps with frozen shoulder because they contain bioactive compounds that target inflammation and promote relaxation. The teas' anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and stiffness in the affected area, while their relaxing effects ease tension in the muscles and joints. Additionally, the teas' ability to improve blood circulation can help restore mobility and flexibility to the affected arm, alleviating the pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties: The alkaloids present in Chinese Birthwort tea, such as aristolochic acid, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, which can be beneficial in reducing stiffness and discomfort associated with Frozen Shoulder.

Blood stasis-relieving properties: Chinese Birthwort is believed to help resolve blood stagnation and promote circulation, which may aid in alleviating numbness, tingling, and limited mobility characteristic of Frozen Shoulder.

Anti-arthritic and anti-rheumatic properties: The tea's active compounds have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce joint pain, stiffness, and inflammation associated with Frozen Shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help alleviate pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Buds: Contain potent flavonoids and terpenes that reduce swelling and promote blood circulation, aiding in the recovery process.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried aristolochia manshuriensis herbs in a heat-resistant container and measure out 15 grams per cup of water.
Boil filtered water in a kettle or pot and pour it over the herb mixture in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes to release the bioactive compounds and flavonoids into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or mug, discarding the solids.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before drinking it immediately to alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric teas helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and stiffness in the affected joints. The curcuminoids present in turmeric have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties can help protect against oxidative damage caused by free radicals, further enhancing its therapeutic effects on frozen shoulder symptoms such as limited mobility and pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric tea's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and pain in the shoulder joint, which can alleviate stiffness and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds in turmeric tea, such as curcuminoids, help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting overall health and reducing tissue damage that may contribute to frozen shoulder symptoms.

Pain relief: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has been shown to have analgesic and anti-nociceptive properties, making it an effective natural pain reliever for the sharp pains associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of turmeric contain a high concentration of curcumin, which helps reduce inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder.
Roots: Turmeric roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate stiffness and limited mobility in the shoulders caused by frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried turmeric powder and 1 cup of boiling water to create the tea mixture.
Mix 1/4 teaspoon of ginger powder into the turmeric powder for added anti-inflammatory benefits.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea and enhance its bioavailability.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-7 minutes before straining it through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Enjoy the warm, comforting tea within 10-15 minutes after preparation for optimal benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with frozen shoulder because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that reduces inflammation and eases pain. The anti-inflammatory properties of licorice tea can help alleviate stiffness and swelling in the affected joint, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving symptoms associated with frozen shoulder. Additionally, licorice tea has been shown to improve range of motion and reduce discomfort, allowing individuals to regain mobility and flexibility in their shoulders.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root has anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and glabridin, which can help reduce inflammation and pain in the affected joint.
Antioxidant properties: Licorice root contains antioxidants that can help neutralize free radicals and protect against oxidative stress, which may contribute to the development of frozen shoulder.
Analgesic and anesthetic properties: Licorice root has been shown to have analgesic and anesthetic effects, which can help relieve pain and numbness associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a common ingredient in medicinal teas as they contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with frozen shoulder.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, also known as the underground stem, is used to make teas that promote joint mobility and relieve stiffness caused by frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of dried licorice root and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 seconds.
Heat one cup of water in a medium-sized pot over low heat for 5 minutes, allowing it to reach a gentle simmer.
Add the ground licorice powder to the hot water, stirring continuously for 1 minute to prevent lumps from forming.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbal properties of the licorice root to infuse into the tea for optimal benefits.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and serving hot as needed, up to three times daily for relief from frozen shoulder symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony teas helps with frozen shoulder because it contains potent anti-inflammatory compounds that effectively reduce pain and stiffness in the affected joint. The tea's warm, soothing properties also promote relaxation and ease muscle tension, which can contribute to frozen shoulder symptoms. Additionally, the antioxidants present in the tea help to protect the joint from further damage, promoting a faster recovery. By incorporating Chinese peony teas into their daily routine, individuals with frozen shoulder may experience significant relief from discomfort and improved overall mobility.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chinese Peony tea contains compounds like paeonol, which has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce pain and swelling associated with Frozen Shoulder.

Pain-relieving properties: The tea's active constituents, including alkaloids and glycosides, have analgesic and sedative effects, helping to alleviate discomfort and stiffness in the shoulder region.
Antioxidant properties: Chinese Peony tea contains antioxidants that can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which may contribute to Frozen Shoulder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are considered a key component in treating frozen shoulder due to their ability to relieve pain and reduce inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes help to alleviate stiffness and improve joint mobility, making them an essential part of the tea formula.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried paeonia lactiflora roots and one teaspoon of dried angelica sinensis roots.
Heat two cups of water in a pot until it reaches boiling point then reduce heat to low.
Add the measured roots to the pot and let them steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture into a cup and discard the solids immediately after straining.
Let the tea cool down to room temperature before drinking it slowly throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese foxglove teas helps with frozen shoulder because it is rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that target the affected joint, reducing stiffness and pain. The herbal tea's analgesic properties also help to alleviate discomfort and promote relaxation, allowing for improved mobility and range of motion. Additionally, Chinese foxglove's ability to stimulate blood flow and reduce inflammation can help to break down adhesions and scar tissue, promoting healing and reducing the risk of further injury.
remedy_properties: Turmeric (Curcuma longa): Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Ginger (Zingiber officinale): Analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate pain and stiffness in the affected area.
Cayenne pepper (Capsicum annuum): Counterirritant and analgesic properties, which may help reduce pain by stimulating blood flow to the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Rehmannia glutinosa contain starch, sugars, and various nutrients that help reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with frozen shoulder.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the plant have been used to treat rheumatic diseases, including frozen shoulder, by reducing swelling and improving joint mobility.
remedy_recipe: Gather rehmannia glutinosa roots 100-200 grams dried or fresh and rinse them thoroughly under running water.
Boil filtered water 4 cups in a large pot for 10-15 minutes to create a strong stock.
Add the cleaned rehmannia glutinosa roots to the boiling water stir gently to ensure they are fully submerged.
Simmer the mixture on low heat for 30-40 minutes or until the liquid reaches 2 cups let it cool.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle store in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap teas helps with frozen shoulder because it contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce pain and stiffness in the affected joint. The tea also relaxes muscles and tendons, allowing for greater range of motion and reducing muscle spasms. Additionally, skullcap's sedative effects can help alleviate insomnia and anxiety, common symptoms associated with frozen shoulder, promoting a more restful night's sleep and improved overall well-being. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Skullcap tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as escin, can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with frozen shoulder by decreasing swelling and tenderness in the affected area.

Muscle relaxant properties: The flavonoids present in skullcap tea can help relax muscles and reduce tension, which is essential for alleviating the stiffness and rigidity commonly experienced in frozen shoulder.

Antioxidant properties: Skullcap tea's high antioxidant content helps protect against oxidative stress and damage to tissues, which may contribute to the development of frozen shoulder by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, which help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making them effective for relieving frozen shoulder pain.
Rhyzomes: Contains sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce stiffness and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried scutellaria baicalensis herb per eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the herbs in hot water for 5-7 minutes to allow for optimal flavonoid extraction.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, as it should be served warm rather than hot.
Consume two to three cups of tea per day for at least four weeks to achieve desired therapeutic effects.

intro_desc: Herbal teas for frozen shoulder are a natural remedy that has been used to alleviate symptoms associated with adhesive capsulitis, also known as frozen shoulder. These teas help by reducing inflammation and stiffness in the affected joints, allowing for greater mobility and relief from pain. Examples of herbal teas that have shown promise include turmeric tea, ginger tea, and willow bark tea, which contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. By incorporating these teas into their daily routine, individuals with frozen shoulder may experience improved range of motion and reduced pain, allowing them to enjoy greater flexibility and independence in their daily activities.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat frozen shoulder with herbal teas is to consume warm beverages containing turmeric, ginger, and willow bark. Turmeric's anti-inflammatory properties reduce pain and swelling, while ginger's warming effects increase blood flow to the affected area. Willow bark's salicylic acid helps alleviate stiffness and discomfort. Drink 2-3 cups daily for several weeks to experience relief from frozen shoulder symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to frozen shoulder that are treated with herbal teas are arthritis, tendonitis, and bursitis. Herbal teas such as turmeric, ginger, and devil's claw have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling. Teas made from willow bark, meadowsweet, and valerian root can also provide relief from muscle spasms and stiffness associated with these conditions. Drinking these herbal teas regularly may help alleviate symptoms and promote overall joint health.
